name: Ministorum Sermon

src.description: While this unit contains a MINISTORUM PRIEST, each time a model in this unit makes a melee attack, add 1 to the Wound roll.

note: Only applies to melee, not all weapons; requires Ministorum Priest unit membership.

name: Cherub

src.description:
Once per battle, you can target this unit with the Command Re-roll Stratagem for 0CP, and can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.

Designer’s Note:Place a Cherub token next to the unit, removing it once this ability has been used.

note: Vague 'gain ? CP'; actually gains Miracle dice, not CP. Triggered by Act of Faith, not generic.

name: Simulacrum Imperialis

src.description: Improve the Leadership characteristic of models in the bearer’s unit by 1.

note: Completely wrong: describes Miracle dice generation on objective control, not Ld boost.

name: Attached Unit

src.description: If a MINISTORUM PRIEST from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a BATTLE SISTERS SQUAD, it can be attached to this unit instead. If a MINISTORUM PRIEST from your army is attached to this unit during the Declare Battle Formations step, that model gains the Scouts 6"ability.

note: Vague 'attached unit eligibility'; misses Ministorum Priest attachment rules and Scouts 6 grant.

name: Atttached Unit

src.description: If a MINISTORUM PRIEST or INQUISITOR model from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a SISTERS OF BATTLE SQUAD, it can be attached to this unit instead. If a MINISTORUM PRIEST or INQUISITOR model from your army is attached to this unit during the Declare Battle Formations step, that model gains the Scouts 6"ability.

note: Vague 'attached unit eligibility'; misses Ministorum Priest/Inquisitor options and Scouts 6 grant.

name: Leader

src.description: Before the battle, CHARACTER units with the Leader ability can be attached to one of their Bodyguard units to form an Attached unit.

note: Missing core mechanism: CHARACTER units attach to Bodyguard units forming attached units; generated loses the attachment formation constraint.

name: Unstoppable Champion

src.description: The first time this model is destroyed, roll one D6 at the end of the phase. On a 2+, set this model back up on the battlefield, as close as possible to where it was destroyed and not within Engagement Range of any enemy units, with 1 wound remaining.

note: Correct roll and outcome but misses placement constraint (close as possible, not within Engagement Range).

name: Firing Deck 2

src.description: Each time this TRANSPORT shoots, select one weapon from up to 2 of models embarked within it whose units have not shot this phase; this TRANSPORT counts as being equipped with those weapons as well.

note: Generated misses core mechanic: selecting embarked models' weapons; doesn't convey what firing-deck actually does.

name: Rapid Deployment

src.description: Units can disembark from this TRANSPORT after it has Advanced. Units that do so count as having made a Normal move that phase, and cannot declare a charge in the same turn, but can otherwise act normally.

note: Generated oversimplifies; missing disembark-after-advance, normal-move-count, charge ineligibility logic.

name: Fights First

src.description: Units with this ability that are eligible to fight do so in the Fights First step, provided every model in the unit has this ability.

note: Missing qualification: only if every model in unit has the ability.

name: Lone Operative

src.description: Unless part of an Attached unit, this unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 12".

note: Misses Attached unit clause; rule applies unless part of attached unit.

name: Acrobatic Escape

src.description: At the end of the Fight phase, if this model is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Fall Back move of up to D6". In addition, at the end of your opponent’s turn, if this model is not within 3"of one or more enemy units, you can remove it from the battlefield and then, in the Reinforcements step of your next Movement phase, set it up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9"horizontally away from all enemy models. If the battle ends and this model is not on the battlefield, it is destroyed.

note: Generated greatly oversimplifies; official has fall-back-and-fight at end of fight phase plus removal/reinforcement cycle.

name: Lord of Deceit (Aura)

src.description: Each time your opponent targets a unit from their army with a Stratagem, if that unit is within 12"of this model, increase the cost of that use of that Stratagem by 1CP.

note: Condition 'has A' is cryptic; missing stratagem-targeting context; should describe CP cost increase.

name: Shadow Assignment

src.description:
This model cannot be selected to be your WARLORD.

If your army faction is AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM, then during the Declare Battle Formations step, you can replace this model with a different OFFICIO ASSASSINORUM model, provided the total points value of the new model does not exceed the points value of the model it replaced. Your army cannot include duplicates of the same model (i.e. after replacing a model with this rule, your army cannot have more than 1 VINDICARE Assassin, it cannot have more than 1 CULEXUS ASSASSIN, it cannot have more than 1 EVERSOR ASSASSIN and it cannot have more than 1 CALLIDUS ASSASSIN).

note: Generated vastly simplifies; official covers warlord restriction, faction-gated model replacement, and duplicate limits.

name: Authority of the Inquisition

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, it can embark within any TRANSPORT that its Bodyguard unit can embark within.

note: Generated abstraction 'special embark rule' obscures the core mechanic: transport-agnostic embarking.

name: No Mercy

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes an attack that targets a unit that is Below Half‐strength, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Condition 'at once per battle' incorrect; rule triggers on below-half-strength targets each attack.

name: Purge and Cleanse

src.description: Each time this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the phase, that enemy unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover.

note: Generated reverses effect: should deny cover after shooting, not grant it; misses target-selection requirement.

name: Loyal Henchmen

src.description: While an INQUISITOR model is leading this unit, each time an attack targets this unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.

note: Partially correct but misses INQUISITOR leadership requirement; not automatic for all units.

name: Tome‐skull

src.description: Once per battle for each Tome‐skull this unit is equipped with, at the start of any phase, you can select either one friendly Agents of the Imperium unit that is Battle‐shocked and within 6"of this unit or one enemy unit within 6"of this unit. If you select a friendly unit, that unit is no longer Battle‐shocked. If you select an enemy unit, it must take a Battle‐shock test.

note: Missing dual-use mechanic (remove battle shock OR force test); oversimplifies once-per-battle-per-item.

name: Inquisitorial Henchmen

src.description: If your Army Faction is not AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM, then for each INQUISITOR unit you include in your army, you can include one INQUISITORIAL AGENTS unit in your army that does not count towards the number of RETINUE units your army can include (see Assigned Agents)

note: Generated leaves out faction condition (non-agents armies only) and retinue-exemption detail.

name: Power of the Rosette

src.description: Each time you target this model’s unit with a Stratagem, roll one D6:on a 3+, you gain 1CP.

note: Misses stratagem-targeting trigger and the 3+ roll gate on CP gain.

name: Blessed Wardings

src.description: While the bearer is leading a unit, models in that unit have a 6+ invulnerable save.

note: Shows ?+ (unknown value) instead of concrete 6+ invulnerable save.

name: Psychic Gifts

src.description: The bearer has the PSYKER keyword.

note: Generated says 'weapons gain Psyker' but official gives PSYKER keyword to model, not weapons.

name: Hammerhand (Psychic)

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a Charge move, until the end of the turn, melee weapons equipped by models in this unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Missing charge-trigger; rule applies only after charge moves, not permanently.

name: Narthecium

src.description: In your Command phase, you can return 1 destroyed model (excluding CHARACTERS) to the bearer’s unit.

note: Missing EXCLUDING CHARACTERS clause and command-phase timing.

name: Rites of Teleportation

src.description: If one or more INQUISITOR units are attached to this unit during the Declare Battle formations step, models in those units have the Deep Strike ability.

note: Generated fails to convey: deep strike applies only to attached INQUISITOR units during formations declare.

name: Masters of Close Confines

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets the closest eligible target, that attack has the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Missing 'closest eligible target' constraint; scope says permanent but rule applies per ranged attack.

name: Navy Bodyguard

src.description: If your Army Faction is not AGENTS OF THE IMPERIUM, then for each VOIDFARERS CHARACTER unit you include in your army, you can include one VOIDSMEN-AT-ARMS unit in your army that does not count towards the number of RETINUE units your army can include (see Assigned Agents).

note: Missing faction condition (non-agents only) and retinue-exemption detail.

name: Death to the Alien

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, re‐roll a Hit roll of 1. If the target of that attack does not have the IMPERIUM or CHAOS keywords, you can re‐roll the Hit roll instead.

note: Generated oversimplifies conditional reroll; official has ones-reroll plus non-IMPERIUM/CHAOS full-reroll.

name: Frenzon

src.description: This model is eligible to shoot and declare a charge in a turn in which it Advanced.

note: Missing trigger (Advanced) and scope context; says action-eligible but means shoot-and-charge-eligible.

name: Overkill

src.description: Once per battle, in your Movement phase, this model can use this ability before it makes a Normal move. If it does, until the end of the turn, add 6"to this model’s Move characteristic and add 3 to the Attacks characteristic of this model’s melee weapons.

note: Generated shows only attack bonus; misses once-per-battle activation and move bonus.

name: Kill Team

src.description:
Each time an attack targets this unit, if it contains models with different Toughness characteristics, until the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, use the Toughness characteristic of the majority of the models in that unit when determining what roll is required for that attack to successfully wound. If two or more Toughness characteristics are tied for majority, use the highest value.

For the purposes of determining which models in this unit can embark within a TRANSPORT, Gravis Veteran models take up the space of 2 models, but can otherwise embark within any TRANSPORT their unit can embark within, even though similar models in other units have the GRAVIS keyword.

Designer’s Note: While the abstractions in the above rule cause some models to behave differently to similar models in other units, they are designed to minimise complicated Transport rules.

note: Generated completely misses mechanic: mixed-toughness majority logic and Gravis-space-exception rule.

name: Stealth

src.description: If every model in a unit has this ability, then each time a ranged attack is made against it, subtract 1 from that attack's Hit roll.

note: Missing 'every model' qualifier; applies only if full unit has ability.

name: Dead‐shot

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack, when selecting targets for that attack, you can ignore the Lone Operative ability.

note: Missing ranged-attack context; 'ignores lone operative' is correct but narrowly scoped.

name: Shieldbreaker

src.description: Once per battle, when selecting targets for this model’s exitus rifle, it can fire a shieldbreaker round. If it does, until the end of the phase, each time this model makes an attack with that weapon, add 1 to the Wound roll and any successful Wound roll scores a Critical Wound.

note: Missing once-per-battle, shieldbreaker-round selector, and critical wound on successful roll.

name: Imperial Law

src.description: At the start of the battle, select one unit from your opponent’s army. Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets that unit, that attack has the [LETHAL HITS] and [PRECISION] abilities.

note: Missing 'select one enemy unit at battle start' targeting constraint.

name: Nuncio-acquila

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of any Command phase, you can select one objective marker within 6” of the bearer. All enemy units (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES) within range of that objective marker must take a Battle‐shock test. Each objective marker can only be targeted by this ability once per turn.

note: Generated loses details: once-per-battle, objective-marker selection, MONSTER/VEHICLE exemptions, one-per-turn.

name: Arbites Medi-kit

src.description: At the start of your Command phase, if the bearer’s unit is below its Starting Strength, you can return up to D3 destroyed Exaction Vigilants to this unit.

note: Missing specific unit type (Exaction Vigilants) and below-starting-strength condition.

name: Breaching Team

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, re-roll a Wound roll of 1. If the target of that attack is an enemy unit within range of an objective marker, you can re-roll the Wound roll instead.

note: Reroll condition correct but misses objective-marker-range bonus clause for full reroll.

name: Gheistskull

src.description: Once per battle, when you select this unit as the target of the Grenade Stratagem, you can target one enemy unit visible to and within 18"of this unit that is not within Engagement Range of any units from your army, instead of one within 8".

note: Missing grenade-stratagem context; generated loses stratagem interaction and range details.

name: CAT Unit

src.description:
Once per battle, when this unit is selected to shoot, until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in this unit gain the [IGNORES COVER] ability.

Designer’s Note:Place one Gheistskull and one CAT Unit token next to this unit, removing each token once the relevant ability has been used.

note: Correct ability but missing once-per-battle gating and designer token-tracking note.

name: Endurant Shield

src.description: The bearer has a 4+ invulnerable save.

note: Shows ?+ instead of concrete 4+ invulnerable save.

name: Etheric Emergence

src.description: In your Movement phase when this model is set up on the battlefield using the Deep Strike ability, it can perform an etheric emergence. If it does, this model can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 6"horizontally away from all enemy units, but until the end of the turn, it is not eligible to declare a charge.

note: Correct choice structure but misses 6" placement range and movement-phase-only timing.

name: Abomination

src.description: This model has the Feel No Pain 2+ ability against Psychic Attacks.

note: Generated says feel-no-pain 2+ universally but official restricts to Psychic Attacks only.

name: Soulless Horror (Aura)

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of any Command phase, this model can use this ability. If it does, each enemy unit within 9"of this model must take a Battle‐shock test, subtracting 1 from that test (or subtracting 2 if that unit is a PSYKER).

note: Missing PSYKER subtractive modifier (-2 vs -1); generated loses conditional malus.

name: Merciless Judgement

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that is Below Half‐strength, add 1 to the Wound roll.

note: Generated adds 'shooting phase' condition; official applies to all ranged attacks during shooting.

name: Dedication to Duty

src.description: Each time a model in this unit is destroyed by a melee attack, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6:on a 4+, do not remove it from play. The destroyed model can fight after the attacking model’s unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.

note: Missing D6 roll gate (4+) and post-attack-timing; generated oversimplifies fight-on-death.

name: Third Eye (Psychic)

src.description: At the start of your Shooting phase, select one enemy unit within 12"of and visible to this model. That unit must take a Battle‐shock test, subtracting 2 from the result if it is an INFANTRY unit. If the test is failed, that enemy unit suffers 3 mortal wounds.

note: Generated misses trigger (start of shooting phase), battle-shock test, and conditional damage on failure.

name: Xenos Hunter

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit that does not have the IMPERIUM or CHAOS keywords, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Correct but misses non-IMPERIUM/CHAOS keyword condition.

name: Psychic Veil (Psychic)

src.description: In your Command phase, this PSYKER can use this ability. If it does, roll one D6:on a 1, this PSYKER’s unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 2+, until the start of your next Command phase, this PSYKER’s unit can only be selected as the target of a ranged attack if the attacking model is within 18".

note: Generated confuses scope and damage; official has D3-risk-on-1 plus 18"-range restriction-on-2+.

name: Malefic Wardings (Psychic)

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, models in that unit have a 6+ invulnerable save, and a 4+ invulnerable save against Psychic Attacks and attacks made by DAEMON models.

note: Shows ?+ instead of concrete 6+ base invulnerable save; misses 4+ against Psychic/Daemon attacks.

name: Spy Network

src.description: Each time your opponent gains a CP as the result of an ability, roll one D6:on a 2+, you also gain 1CP.

note: Describes gaining CP; actually triggers when opponent gains CP. Omits conditional trigger entirely.

name: Glovodan Psyber‐eagle

src.description: In your Command phase, you can select one enemy unit within 18"of the bearer. Until the start of your next Command phase, that unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover.

note: Says unit gains cover; actually *denies* enemy unit benefit of cover. Reverses the mechanic.

name: Defenders of the Faith

src.description: At the end of your Command phase, if this unit is within range of an objective marker you control, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase.

note: "Modify OC by ?" is unintelligible. Actual text is about objective control persistence conditions.

name: Incensor Cherub

src.description:
Once per battle, you can target this unit with the Command Re‐roll Stratagem for 0CP, and can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.

Designer’s Note:Place an Incensor Cherub token next to the unit, removing it once this ability has been used.

note: "Refund ? CP" is vague. Actually allows free Command Re-roll use with specific restrictions.

name: Hover

src.description: When you are instructed to Declare Battle Formations, you can declare this model will be in Hover mode. Its Move characteristic changes to 20" and it loses the AIRCRAFT keyword.

note: Omits requirement to declare hover mode; misses aircraft keyword loss.

name: Blackstar Cluster Launcher

src.description: Each time this model ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move and roll six D6:for each 5+, that unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Claims permanent effect; actually triggers on normal moves, rolls against each model overrun.

name: Auspex Array

src.description: Ranged weapons equipped by the bearer have the [IGNORES COVER] ability.

note: Correctly conveys ignores cover; limits to ranged weapons, which is correct.

name: Infernum Halo-launcher

src.description: The bearer has the SMOKE keyword.

note: Says weapons gain smoke; actually bearer gains smoke keyword. Different mechanic entirely.

name: Zealot

src.description: Once per battle, in the Fight phase, this model can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the phase, improve the Attacks and Strength characteristics of melee weapons equipped by this model by 3.

note: Correctly captures improvements; misses "once per battle" restriction and applies to unit instead of just model.

name: Holy Mission

src.description: If this model is attached to a DOMINION SQUAD during the Declare Battle Formations step, it gains the Scouts 6"ability. If this model is attached to a SISTERS NOVITIATE SQUAD during the Declare Battle Formations step, it gains the Infiltrators ability.

note: "Unit gains attached unit eligibility" is nonsensical. Actually grants scouts/infiltrators based on target unit type.

name: Holy Piety

src.description: Each time this model makes a melee attack, unless this model’s unit is Battle-shocked, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

note: Re-rolls as permanent; actually conditional on model not being battle-shocked. Misses condition.

name: Backroom Deals

src.description: If your army contains one or more units with this ability, during the Declare Battle Formations step, select one of those units. While the selected unit is leading a unit, models in that unit have the Infiltrators ability.

note: "Modify stats" is too vague. Actually affects attached unit's infiltrator ability.

name: Warrant of Trade

src.description: If your army includes one or more units with this ability, after both players have deployed their armies, select up to D3 IMPERIUM BATTLELINE units from your army and redeploy them. When doing so, you can set those units up in Strategic Reserves, regardless of how many units are already in Strategic Reserves.

note: "Modify stats" is meaningless. Actually redeployment of imperium battleline into reserves.

name: that redeploy units, roll off

src.description: the winner chooses who redeploys units first.

note: Overly verbose phrasing; context-dependent mechanic not fully clear without deployment knowledge.

name: Evade and Survive

src.description: Once per turn, when an enemy unit ends a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move within 9" of this model, if this model is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can make a Normal move.

note: "Reactive move" loses specificity; misses once-per-turn, non-engagement-range, and charge-block restrictions.

name: Assigned Agents
note: "Self gains faction metadata" is meaningless. Describes army composition and detachment selection rules.
